page: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an Intel Xeon 8375C running at 3.5 GHz, equipped with a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and cryptographic capabilities. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in the PassMark CPU score, extended instructions, and encryption tests. Multi-core performance is further validated by strong results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 6623.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 333.99 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests, falls within the average tier.

The instance exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs in specialized workloads. While LLM prompt processing speeds for small and medium models are strong, text generation speeds for larger models, such as the 70B parameter model, are weak, registering at 2.75 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its high core density, 50 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong database operation benchmarks, this instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network speed of 50 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in PassMark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. However, single-core benchmarks and large-scale LLM text generation represent notable performance bottlenecks. The instance is suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose x86_64 instance offering balanced memory and strong multi-core compute capabilities for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM on Intel Xeon 8375C processors
Top-tier cryptographic and extended instruction benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and web serving
